papers.title: Rethinking Muon Beyond Pretraining: Spectral Failures and High-Pass Remedies for VLA and RLVR
papers.abstract_snippet: Muon is a matrix-aware optimizer that leverages Newton-Schulz (NS) iterations to enforce spectral gradient orthogonalization by driving all singular values of the momentum matrix toward 1. While this uniform spectral whitening enhances exploration and outperforms AdamW in LLM pretraining, we show it could lead to fundamental limitations beyond pretraining in two regimes: (i) cross-modality vision-language-action (VLA) training, where inherently low-rank action-module gradients cause amplificatio
papers.reason: 针对 VLA 和 RLVR 训练中的谱失效问题，提出基于高通过滤的 Muon 优化器改进方案。为提升 VLA 训练稳定性和收敛速度提供了可复用的优化器配置建议。

papers.title: Distributionally Robust Control via Stein Variational Inference for Contact-Rich Manipulation
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2605.19029v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reliable robotic manipulation requires control policies that can accurately represent and adapt to uncertainty arising from contact-rich interactions. papers.title: Automatically Improving Simulation Physics for Articulated Objects

papers.title: COBALT: Crowdsourcing Robot Learning via Cloud-Based Teleoperation with Smartphones

papers.title: DEFLECT: Delay-Robust Execution via Flow-matching Likelihood-Estimated Counterfactual Tuning for VLA Policies
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2605.19294v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) policies are typically deployed with asynchronous inference: the robot executes a previously predicted action chunk while the model computes the next one. papers.title: SafeAlign-VLA: A Negative-Enhanced Safe Alignment Framework for Risk-Aware Autonomous Driving
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2605.19524v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: End-to-end autonomous driving systems excel in common scenarios but struggle with safety-critical long-tail cases. papers.title: Implicit Action Chunking for Smooth Continuous Control

papers.title: Preserving Foundational Capabilities in Flow-Matching VLAs through Conservative SFT
